title: andropogon nardus subsp. grandis
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. grandis, commonly known as the sandhill grass or large spear grass, is a subspecies of Andropogon nardus, a perennial grass native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Andropogon nardus subsp. grandis include its ability to aid in respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
